# @hoge1e3/immer-path-ref

Wraps a `Ref<T>` from `@hoge1e3/ref` with [immer](https://immerjs.github.io/immer/)-powered deep path access, enabling reactive reads and writes to nested properties of an immutable state tree.

This allows you to pass a narrowed `PathRef<T[K]>` into a child component, which can read and write that specific nested value — without the child needing to know about the full parent state.

## Installation

```sh
npm install @hoge1e3/immer-path-ref
```

## API

### `create<T>(r: Ref<T>): PathRef<T>`

Wraps a root `Ref<T>` as a `PathRef<T>`. The returned `PathRef` has the same value as the original ref and exposes a `.join()` method for path traversal.

### `PathRef<T>` / `ReadonlyPathRef<T>`

Both extend `Ref<T>` / `ReadonlyRef<T>` with an additional `.join()` method:

- **`.join(key: string | number): PathRef<T[key]>`** — Returns a new `PathRef` pointing to the `key` property of the current value. Writing to the child `PathRef` triggers an immer `produce()` on the root ref, preserving immutability at every level.

- **`.join(fn: (t: T) => N): ReadonlyPathRef<N>`** — Returns a read-only derived ref computed by applying `fn` to the current value. Cannot be written to (throws if attempted).

Change events propagate both ways:
- When the root ref changes, child `PathRef`s fire their own `"change"` event if their slice of the value changed.
- When a child `PathRef` is written to, the root ref is updated via immer and fires its own `"change"` event.

## Usage

### Basic path traversal

```js
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";
import { create } from "@hoge1e3/immer-path-ref";

const state = ref({
  user: {
    name: "Alice",
    age: 30,
  },
  todos: ["Buy milk", "Write docs"],
});

const stateRef = create(state);

// Drill down into nested properties
const nameRef = stateRef.join("user").join("name");
console.log(nameRef.value); // "Alice"

// Writing updates the root immutably via immer
nameRef.value = "Bob";
console.log(state.value.user.name); // "Bob"
console.log(state.value.user.age);  // 30 (unchanged)
```

### Reactive change propagation

```js
nameRef.on("change", ({ prev, val }) => {
  console.log(`name: ${prev} -> ${val}`);
});

nameRef.value = "Carol"; // logs: "name: Bob -> Carol"
```

### Read-only derived path

```js
import { findById } from "@acepad/todo";

// Derive a ref to a specific item by ID (read-only, since fn path)
const itemRef = rootRef.join((root) => findById(root, someId));
console.log(itemRef.value.title);
// itemRef.value = ...; // throws: "Read only path!"
```

### Passing to child components

```js
function todoComponent(itemRef, expandedRef) {
  return c(wid[itemRef.value.id], itemRef, expandedRef, (item, expanded) =>
    t.li(
      t.input({ type: "checkbox", checked: item.ok }),
      t.span(item.title),
      expanded ? subListComponent(itemRef.join("sub")) : []
    )
  );
}
```

## Notes

- Path refs share the same root `Ref` — writing to any child always updates the single root.
- Equality checks use `===` on the path's resolved value; unchanged slices do not fire events.
- Read-only paths (created with a function) throw `"Read only path!"` if you attempt to write.
- Dependencies: `@hoge1e3/ref`, [immer](https://immerjs.github.io/immer/)
